Rachel rolls well; and the attack hits with Margin of Success of seven. Instead of adding [0 the damn- age rolled, that number of dice I5 added t0 the dam age dice for the wcapon_ However, since her character only fired shots this burst, Rachel can only add dice of damage The extra MoS of 2 IS disregarded,
SPLASH DAMAGE Some attacks such as flamers spread their effects over wide area splashing over more than one part of the target: Such attacks are noted as splash in the weapon profiles These weapons have specific effects if using the Hit Location rules (p 121). Under the standard rules hits from splash weapons are worked oul normally except the armor penetration the weapon IS increased by to represent the weapon" aility to work its way around armor and strike unprotected areas: This does not apply if the target wearing full-coverage armor such as battle armor:
BUPPRESSING FIRE Weapons capable of burst fire can be used to saturate a area with projectiles in the hopes of hitting several targets simply discouraging the enemy'$ advance into the area of fire Such attacks are incredibly inaccurate and consume Iarge quan- tltles of ammunition; but they can be effective against massed formations of enemy Or as a intimidation tactic; suppressing-fire attack Is Complex Action. The attacker must expend shots per 1-meter-long line' to fill with weapon the length of the line limited by the weapon'$ maximum rate of fire. Then make separate attack roll against every char- xter along the line of attack; as well as any character wno enters crosses the line ot attack before the attacking char acter" next phase begins_ Apply a additional +2 Modifier t0 the norma burst-fire  modifier   fortnese attacks, but resolve eacn standard single-shot attack Ki e . Margin Success adds directly t0 damage rather Inan adding dice)
INCENDIARY WEAPONS AND FIRE Characters attacked flamers other incendiary weapons can set on fire Also moving through burning area might cause character be set alight with the same effect. Such unfortunates will suffer automatic flame attacks every turn until they are put out or the fire burns itself out: Each time character is hit by a incendiary weapon, the character i5 set on fire any of the dice rolled for damage came up as 65_ The character will burn for number of turns equal t0 the number of 6s rolled for damage, including any additional 6s generated from open-ended results. Moving through burning area sets character on fire on 106 roll of At the end of each turn burning character takes an auto matic energy weapon (E) hit with AP and damage equal to the weapon that set the fire originally: (In the case of natural fires_ use the man-pack flamer AP and damage.) Since there IS no attack roll. there chance for increased damage due to Mos burning character also very distracted by the pain and smoke created Dy tne fire _ suffering target number modifiers for Bad Visibility and Bad Conditions_ total TN Modifier 0f +5_ Of course burning character may want tO put himself out. This Complex Action and automatically successful: However; the character ends the action in prone position and suffers Fatigue from the vigorous effort: character within meter of the burning character may extinguish the fire ne has fire extinguisher or other appropriate equipment. This is also Complex Action but it is only automatically successful if using fire extinguisher. Using sand water will only work half the time (on 106 roll of or better), SPECIAL SITUATIONS Certain  special situations can come up in combat that require additional rules Note that some of these rules are con- sidered optional. Tney enhance realism or detail at the expense of simplicity ad should only be used players and the gamemaster agree that they are worth the extra effort;
Kar 5 character Jack Hammer wants t0 fll meter-wide corridor with bullets coverhis team '$ retreat: He $ firing a automatic rifle that rated at burst (15/31. He will need to fire 10 shots to cover the meters 10-shot burst from this weapon has Recoil Modifier (10 3 = 3.3 rounded up t0 41. plus an additional +2 for suppressing fire. There are thugs standing the area 0f effect at the time the attack initiated; So Karl makes separate attack against each of them with tne +6 Modifier (plus all other relevant modifiers). Each attack that hits resoived as stan dard single-shot   attack; any additional   characters enter cross the line Of fire before Jack starts his next phase, they would each be attacked:
INDIRECT FIRE Some kinds attacks can be launched over intervening obstacles at targets the attacker can" see _ such as grenades and mortars These attacks are called indirect attacks_ there i5 friendly character who has LOS the target; and that character has some Wav communicale witn the attacking character, tnen the friendly character can act as spotter for the attack by spending Complex Action; In this case, the TN has +3 Modifier: there no spotter for an indi" attack; the TN has +6 Modifier.
INANIMATE TAROETS Attacks against inanimate and completely immobile targets are resolved using base TN of since there is no RFL or INT to figure into the attack. Note that this does not apply to char- acter who has simply not moved in lurn but does apply t0
